summary:
Complete bilateral palsy leads to severe impairment of vocal function; patients will have a weak or absent voice along with potential respiratory issues such as stridor and difficulty breathing. The primary issue stems from paralyzed vocal cords that can't move at all, making normal use impossible while also risking airway compromise through inadequate protection mechanisms against choking hazards. Agent2 summarized that:

In complete bilateral palsy of the recurrent laryngeal nerves,
patients lose their voices completely and may struggle to breathe normally due to immobile vocal cords impacting airflow and protecting the airways effectively.
